discovery.summary: Design fully settled by the feature brief: exact constants (BETRAYAL_HURT_POINTS=8 -> BETRAYAL_BONUS=4), the resolver + mirror branches, the viewer chip, the version bump v4->v5, and every doc/test touchpoint are specified. No clarifying questions needed. Carrying two reality-check assumptions into the spec: (1) viewer_win_probs.py no longer exists (deleted with the win-prob overlay) so the only running-score mirror is apply_inround_turn in scoring.py; (2) boardgame-design-patterns.md does not exist in this checkout, so that doc touchpoint has no file to edit.
non_goals: Do NOT change the 12-point relative swing magnitude.
Do NOT change the Team Attack case (two players HURT one victim = -8 total from two independent -4s; that -8 is not a betrayal and must stay).
Do NOT retrain the win-probability models or add an exploiter bot.
Do NOT widen move_effect's contract across other games (liars_dice) unless strictly needed.
acceptance_criteria: Betraying a helper: attacker turn delta = +8 (=+4 help +4 bonus), victim = -4, proven by a resolver test.
Score floor still applied to the FINAL per-player delta, not per-hurt.
Agent-facing GAME_RULES_TEXT states attacker +4 / victim -4, header bumped v4 -> v5.
Viewer shows the betrayal honestly (victim -4, attacker +4); no stale -8 anywhere in the UI.
No stale BETRAYAL_HURT_POINTS references remain anywhere.

checklist.goal: Re-split the betray-a-helper payoff so the attacker gains +4 (BETRAYAL_BONUS) on top of the +4 HELP they receive, while the victim now takes the NORMAL -4 (HURT_POINTS) instead of -8. Net that turn becomes attacker +8 / victim -4; the 12-point relative swing is unchanged, just re-attributed so the attacker rises instead of the victim cratering.
checklist.audience: LLM agents competing in Hoard-Hurt-Help (the agent-facing GAME_RULES_TEXT ships to every agent verbatim) and human spectators watching the replay viewer.
checklist.constraints: Silent-risk: a scoring change passes all tests but can be wrong in prod, and the viewer's running-score mirror (apply_inround_turn) can silently diverge from the authoritative resolver. Score floor must apply to the FINAL per-player delta, not per-hurt. move_effect(action) only receives the action string with no turn context, so the per-move chip cannot by itself tell a HURT is a betrayal. NOTE: prompt referenced viewer_win_probs.py but that file was deleted in the win-prob-overlay removal; the only running-score mirror is apply_inround_turn in scoring.py. NOTE: .claude/skills/game-design/references/boardgame-design-patterns.md does not exist in this checkout.
checklist.silent_risk_note: scoring change can pass tests but be wrong in prod; viewer running-score mirror can silently diverge from resolver
checklist.design_settled_note: exact numbers and touchpoints are fully specified
